arts.ttl: Milan pursue Kolo Muani from Paris Saint-Germain
arts.sum: AC Milan are seeking to strengthen their squad ahead of the January transfer window, with a particular focus on acquiring a new striker. The club are aiming to enhance the options available to head coach Paulo Fonseca, targeting not only a forward but also a central midfielder and a full-back. The post Milan pursue Kolo Muani from Paris Saint-Germain appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Pulisic suffers injury setback, will miss Roma clash
arts.sum: Christian Pulisic, AC Milan's leading scorer, is out of Sunday's Serie A clash against Roma after a setback during his recovery, coach Paulo Fonseca said.

arts.ttl: Fonseca: ‘Pulisic out against Roma, Bennacer will play’
arts.sum: AC Milan manager Paulo Fonseca addressed the media ahead of the crucial year-end clash against Roma, a match that could have significant implications for Milan's ambitions and Fonseca's own position at the club. Despite the usual denials, the Portuguese coach's status remains under scrutiny. The post Fonseca: ‘Pulisic out against Roma, Bennacer will play’ appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Tomori to leave Milan? Ex-Chelsea wonderkid a January opportunity
arts.sum: Until October, Tomori was a regular starter under Paulo Fonseca, but his shaky performances provided the club's other defensive options with opportunities, which were eventually grasped. His stocks are the lowest they have been since he joined Milan and while this may no longer make him a particularly attractive signing, he could be a 'market opportunity' for Premier League clubs.

arts.ttl: Milan’s January pursuit of Ricci: Tomori factor and Torino’s stance
arts.sum: AC Milan have identified Youssouf Fofana and Tijjani Reijnders as the cornerstones of their midfield, but depth remains a concern. The Rossoneri management is now considering a January reinforcement, with Samuele Ricci of Torino emerging as the top target for Giorgio Furlani, Zlatan Ibrahimovic, and Geoffrey Moncada. The post Milan’s January pursuit of Ricci: Tomori factor and Torino’s stance appeared first on FootItalia.com.
